title: Enhance round 2 campaign map with external 2D assets
description: Clone BrianOtaku/2D-TopDown-Game-Assets, reuse its sprite and environment art in the round 2 campaign mode, and increase the map size while keeping gameplay stable.
close_reason: Expanded the round 2 campaign map to 28x28, added imported 2D TopDown sprites and scenery decorations, and verified the build.

title: Add Kenney fantasy textures to campaign map
description: Use the kenney_retro-textures-fantasy asset pack to render the campaign map with texture-based floors, walls, doors, and interactive tiles instead of flat color blocks.
close_reason: Implemented texture-backed campaign map rendering using Kenney fantasy assets and verified with a production build.

title: Improve map readability with clearer element icons
description:
Enhance the campaign map so doors, terminals, items, NPCs, exits, and hazards are easier to distinguish at a glance. Keep the existing detective/noir paper visual language and avoid cyber/hacker styling.

Scope:
- Add clearer element icons/markers for map tiles.
- Improve the board framing and legend/readability.
- Preserve gameplay behavior and existing sprite assets.

Acceptance:
- Doors and other interactables are visually distinct from normal tiles.
- The map still feels like a detective campaign board, not a cyber UI.
- Build passes after the changes.

title: Polish round 2 map UI and swap player icons to character assets
description:
Enhance the round 2 campaign/map presentation without introducing cyber/hacker/digital styling. Replace the existing player markers with the provided character assets, and improve the map/round 2 UI clarity and polish while preserving gameplay behavior.

Scope:
- Use the provided character assets in place of the current player icons on the map.
- Improve round 2 map UI framing, legibility, and HUD presentation.
- Keep the visual direction grounded and non-cyber.

Acceptance:
- Character assets appear as the player/team markers on the map.
- Map and round 2 UI look more intentional and readable.
- No cyber/hacker/digital visual treatment is added.

title: Improve round 2 multiplayer sync and persistence
description:
Round 2 campaign needs stronger two-player handling and better quality-of-life behavior. Current issues include overly narrow campaign state persistence, optimistic session joins with no validation feedback, and weak partner-position/state sync. Also, the server build step currently fails on this Windows environment and should be made reliable.

Scope:
- Make round 2 campaign state persist more of the live progress that matters for multiplayer and refresh recovery.
- Improve session join/create flow so invalid or pending sessions are handled more gracefully.
- Strengthen partner presence/position sync so two-player exploration feels consistent.
- Fix the server build path so 
pm run build completes successfully on Windows.

Acceptance:
- Multiplayer state survives refresh with objective/score/role progress intact.
- Session flow gives clear success/failure feedback instead of assuming joins always work.
- Partner marker sync is stable when either player moves or leaves.
- Production build succeeds locally.

title: Detective Noir Interface Overhaul
description: Unify the entire platform (Board, Evidence Viewer, Profile, Scoreboard, and AI Advisor) into a high-fidelity Pixel-Perfect game aesthetic.

title: Vite Bundle Optimization
description: Optimize chunk sizes to resolve the 500kB minification warning.

value: Project Architecture: Node/Express + Supabase (PostgreSQL) + Socket.IO. UI Aesthetic: 'Detective Noir' high-fidelity game interface. design Tokens: Dark Walnut (#140e06), Bronze (#a07830), Parchment (#f0e0a0). Core mechanisms: Patrick Jane (AI Consultant) and The Adversary (Engagement NPC). Mandatory rebase-push workflow as per AGENTS.md. Session Hand-off: Interface unified (Board, Viewer, Profile, Scoreboard, Advisor). Environment: Windows/PowerShell.
